policies.abstain_policy: If the extracted text is unreadable or empty, assign 0 marks to all criteria and set flag UNREADABLE.

questions.question_text: Explain the time complexity of inserting an element into a balanced BST. Derive the complexity step by step.

criteria.description: States the final complexity is O(log n)
Explains that tree height is log(n) because of the balancing invariant
criteria.partial_credit: Award 1.5 if height is mentioned but balancing invariant is not explained

criteria.description: Walks through the traversal: compare at each node, recurse left/right, insert at leaf
criteria.partial_credit: Award 1 per step correctly described (compare, recurse, insert)

criteria.description: Mentions rebalancing (rotations) after insertion is O(1) amortized
criteria.partial_credit: Award 1 if rotations mentioned but cost not analyzed

deductions.condition: Claims O(n) without specifying this applies only to unbalanced trees
Confuses BST insertion with heap insertion

alternatives.description: Uses AVL or Red-Black tree specific derivation

questions.grader_notes: Diagrams transcribed as textual steps are acceptable.

questions.question_text: Write a function to detect a cycle in a linked list. Explain your approach.

criteria.description: Correctly implements or describes Floyd's two-pointer cycle detection
criteria.partial_credit: Award 2 if hash-set approach used instead — correct but O(n) space

criteria.description: Code handles edge cases: empty list, single node, no-cycle termination
States time O(n) and space O(1) for Floyd's approach

deductions.condition: Code has infinite loop with no termination condition

alternatives.description: Uses hash set instead of Floyd's
alternatives.instruction: Accept but cap Q2_C1 at 2 marks and adjust Q2_C3 to expect O(n) space

questions.grader_notes: Any language accepted. Syntax errors alone should not lose marks if logic is sound.

questions.question_text: What is the time complexity of BFS on a graph with V vertices and E edges?

criteria.partial_credit: Award 1 if only O(V) or only O(E) stated. Award 2 if O(V+E) stated with incorrect justification.
